quiz.title: Unit 5 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Nomenclature and Formula Writing
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Naming Ionic Compounds with Variable Charges, Polyatomic Ions, Molecular Compound Naming, Stock System, IUPAC Nomenclature

questions.question: What is the correct name for PbO₂?
questions.explanation: PbO₂ is named lead(IV) oxide. Oxygen has a -2 charge, so two oxygens give -4. Therefore, lead must have a +4 charge, indicated by (IV).

questions.question: What is the correct name for (NH₄)₂CO₃?
questions.explanation: (NH₄)₂CO₃ is named ammonium carbonate. It contains the ammonium ion (NH₄⁺) and the carbonate ion (CO₃²⁻).

questions.question: Which polyatomic ion has the greatest negative charge?
questions.explanation: PO₄³⁻ (phosphate) has a 3- charge, which is the greatest negative charge among these options. NO₃⁻ is 1-, SO₄²⁻ is 2-, and OH⁻ is 1-.

questions.question: What is the correct formula for iron(III) nitrate?
questions.explanation: Iron(III) nitrate is Fe(NO₃)₃. Iron(III) has a +3 charge (Fe³⁺), and nitrate is NO₃⁻ with a -1 charge, so three nitrates are needed.

questions.question: What is the name of ClO₃⁻?
questions.explanation: ClO₃⁻ is named chlorate. The pattern for chlorine oxyanions is: ClO⁻ (hypochlorite), ClO₂⁻ (chlorite), ClO₃⁻ (chlorate), ClO₄⁻ (perchlorate).

questions.question: What is the correct formula for ammonium chloride?
questions.explanation: Ammonium chloride is NH₄Cl. Ammonium (NH₄⁺) has a +1 charge, and chloride (Cl⁻) has a -1 charge, so they combine in a 1:1 ratio.

questions.question: What is the correct name for Ca(OH)₂?
questions.explanation: Ca(OH)₂ is named calcium hydroxide. It contains the hydroxide ion (OH⁻). Calcium has a +2 charge, so two hydroxide ions are needed.

questions.question: What is the correct formula for aluminum sulfate?
questions.explanation: Aluminum sulfate is Al₂(SO₄)₃. Aluminum has a +3 charge (Al³⁺), and sulfate is SO₄²⁻ with a -2 charge. To balance charges, two Al³⁺ (+6) combine with three SO₄²⁻ (-6).

questions.question: Which of the following gives the correct chemical formula for the compound Magnesium Phosphide?
questions.explanation: Magnesium phosphide is Mg₃P₂. Magnesium (Group 2) forms Mg²⁺, and phosphorus (Group 15) forms P³⁻ (phosphide). To balance charges, three Mg²⁺ (+6) combine with two P³⁻ (-6).

questions.question: Which of the following gives the correct chemical formula for the compound Calcium Nitride?
questions.explanation: Calcium nitride is Ca₃N₂. Calcium (Group 2) forms Ca²⁺, and nitrogen (Group 15) forms N³⁻ (nitride). To balance charges, three Ca²⁺ (+6) combine with two N³⁻ (-6).

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the ionic compound: Fe₃N₂?
questions.explanation: Fe₃N₂ is named iron(II) nitride. Nitrogen (N³⁻) has a -3 charge, so two nitrogens give -6. Therefore, three irons must total +6, meaning each iron has a +2 charge, indicated by (II).

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the ionic compound: CuS?
questions.explanation: CuS is named copper(II) sulfide. Sulfur (S²⁻) has a -2 charge, so copper must have a +2 charge. Copper(I) sulfide would be Cu₂S.

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the molecular compound: CS₂?
questions.explanation: CS₂ is named carbon disulfide. It is a covalent compound, so prefixes are used. The 'di-' prefix indicates two sulfur atoms. The 'mono-' prefix for carbon is omitted.

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the molecular compound: SF₄?
questions.explanation: SF₄ is named sulfur tetrafluoride. It is a covalent compound, so prefixes are used. The 'tetra-' prefix indicates four fluorine atoms. Roman numerals are not used for molecular compounds.

questions.question: Which of these is known as Tetraoxosulfate (VI) acid?
questions.explanation: Sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) is known systematically as tetraoxosulfate(VI) acid. The name indicates a central sulfur atom in the +6 oxidation state bonded to four oxygen atoms (tetraoxo).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered advanced nomenclature and formula writing! 🧪📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review charge balancing for compounds with polyatomic ions and transition metals.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on common polyatomic ions and determining oxidation states.
scoreMessages.0: Please review naming rules for ionic compounds with variable charges, polyatomic ions, and molecular compounds.
